Spiral-1 Modules.subject: Basic Sciences
Spiral-1 Modules.title: Spiral-1: Foundation & Systems-Based Learning (Years 1 & 2)
objectives: Understand the foundational principles of medical sciences.
Integrate knowledge across basic science disciplines.
Apply basic science concepts to clinical scenarios.

Foundation.subject: Basic Sciences
Foundation.title: Cell, Genetics, and Cell Death
objectives: Describe the key organelles and their functions.
Explain the process of DNA transcription and translation.
Differentiate between apoptosis and necrosis.
Analyze different modes of genetic inheritance.

Hematology.subject: Basic Sciences
Hematology.title: Blood, Immunity, Inflammation, Tissue Repair, Antimicrobials, Neoplasia
objectives: Identify different types of blood cells and their roles.
Describe the innate and adaptive immune responses.
Explain the key steps in the inflammatory process.
Understand the basic principles of cancer development.

Locomotion.subject: Basic Sciences
Locomotion.title: Bones, Joints, Nerves, and Muscles
concepts: Skeletal, smooth, and cardiac muscle physiology
objectives: Classify bones and joints based on structure and function.
Explain the sliding filament theory of muscle contraction.
Describe the transmission of nerve impulses.

Respiratory System.subject: Basic Sciences
Respiratory System.title: Respiratory System
objectives: Trace the path of air from the nose to the alveoli.
Explain the principles of gas diffusion across the alveolar-capillary membrane.
Interpret basic spirometry results.
Identify the clinical features of common respiratory conditions.

Cardiovascular System.subject: Basic Sciences
Cardiovascular System.title: Cardiovascular System
objectives: Describe the flow of blood through the heart and body.
Explain the phases of the cardiac cycle and their correlation to the ECG.
Understand the physiological factors that influence blood pressure.
Recognize the pathology of atherosclerosis.

Neurosciences.subject: Basic Sciences
Neurosciences.title: Neurosciences
concepts: Anatomy of the brain and spinal cord
objectives: Identify the major lobes and structures of the brain.
Explain the process of synaptic transmission.
Trace the flow of CSF.
Describe the function of each cranial nerve.

Head, Neck, and Special Senses.subject: Basic Sciences
Head, Neck, and Special Senses.title: Head, Neck, and Special Senses
concepts: Anatomy of the skull, face, and neck
Anatomy and function of the tongue and taste buds
objectives: Identify the major bones and muscles of the head and neck.
Explain how light is focused on the retina.
Describe the process of sound transduction.
Understand the pathways for taste and smell.

Endocrinology.subject: Basic Sciences
Endocrinology.title: Endocrinology
concepts: Insulin and glucagon regulation of glucose
objectives: Name the major endocrine glands and their secreted hormones.
Explain the concept of negative feedback in hormonal regulation.
Describe the pathophysiology of diabetes mellitus.
Discuss the effects of thyroid and adrenal hormones.

Gastrointestinal Tract (GIT).subject: Basic Sciences
Gastrointestinal Tract (GIT).title: GIT and Liver
concepts: Common GI pathologies (e.g., GERD, peptic ulcers)
objectives: Trace the path of food through the digestive system.
Explain the roles of enzymes in digestion.
Describe the metabolic and detoxification functions of the liver.
Understand the mechanisms behind common GI diseases.

Renal and Excretory System.subject: Basic Sciences
Renal and Excretory System.title: Renal and Excretory System
objectives: Identify the key structures of the kidney and nephron.
Explain the process of urine formation.
Describe the role of the kidneys in maintaining acid-base balance.
Understand how RAAS regulates blood pressure.

Reproductive System.subject: Basic Sciences
Reproductive System.title: Reproductive System
objectives: Identify the major organs of the male and female reproductive systems.
Explain the hormonal interactions in the menstrual cycle.
Describe the process of gamete formation.
Understand the key events of fertilization and early pregnancy.

Spiral-2 Modules.subject: Clinical Sciences
Spiral-2 Modules.title: Spiral-2: Clinical Focus (Years 3 & 4)
objectives: Integrate knowledge from basic sciences with clinical presentations.
Develop skills in diagnosing and treating common clinical conditions.
Understand the ethical and legal aspects of patient care.

Foundation II.subject: Clinical Sciences
Foundation II.title: Foundation II
objectives: Explain how drugs are absorbed, distributed, metabolized, and excreted.
Relate cellular and molecular changes to disease symptoms.
Apply biostatistical principles to medical literature.
Discuss the social determinants of health.

Infectious Diseases.subject: Clinical Sciences
Infectious Diseases.title: Infectious Diseases
concepts: Diagnosis and treatment of infectious diseases
objectives: Differentiate between major classes of pathogens.
Describe the host immune response to infection.
Identify the modes of action of various antimicrobial drugs.
Formulate a differential diagnosis for a patient with fever of unknown origin.

Hematology II.subject: Clinical Sciences
Hematology II.title: Hematology II
objectives: Interpret a complete blood count (CBC) and blood smear.
Differentiate between various types of anemia.
Understand the principles of chemotherapy for hematological malignancies.
Identify indications and complications of blood transfusions.

Respiratory System II.subject: Clinical Sciences
Respiratory System II.title: Respiratory System II
concepts: Chest X-ray and CT scan interpretation
Management of asthma and COPD exacerbations
Pneumonia and tuberculosis diagnosis and treatment
objectives: Formulate a differential diagnosis for a patient with shortness of breath.
Interpret a chest radiograph for signs of common diseases.
Develop a treatment plan for an asthma attack.
Recognize the clinical features of tuberculosis.

Cardiovascular System II.subject: Clinical Sciences
Cardiovascular System II.title: Cardiovascular System II
concepts: Diagnosis of myocardial infarction and heart failure
Physical examination findings in heart disease
Management of hypertension and lipid disorders
objectives: Interpret an ECG to identify common arrhythmias.
Understand the mechanisms of action for anti-hypertensive and anti-arrhythmic drugs.
Perform a focused cardiovascular physical exam.
Formulate a treatment plan for a patient with heart failure.

GIT and Liver II.subject: Clinical Sciences
GIT and Liver II.title: GIT and Liver II (including Nutritional Disorders)
concepts: Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) vs. irritable bowel syndrome (IBS)
objectives: Differentiate between various causes of abdominal pain.
Develop a diagnostic workup for GI bleeding.
Understand the clinical and pathological differences between IBD and IBS.
Recognize the complications of liver cirrhosis.

Renal and Excretory System II.subject: Clinical Sciences
Renal and Excretory System II.title: Renal and Excretory System II
concepts: Acute kidney injury (AKI) and chronic kidney disease (CKD)
objectives: Classify and diagnose AKI.
Interpret a urinalysis report.
Manage common electrolyte imbalances.
Describe the principles of hemodialysis and peritoneal dialysis.

Endocrinology II.subject: Clinical Sciences
Endocrinology II.title: Endocrinology II
concepts: Diagnosis and management of diabetes mellitus
Adrenal insufficiency (Addison's disease) and Cushing's syndrome
objectives: Formulate a treatment plan for a patient with type 2 diabetes.
Identify the clinical signs of thyroid dysfunction.
Understand the aetiology and management of adrenal disorders.
Explain how calcium is regulated in the body.

Orthopedics, Rheumatology, Trauma.subject: Clinical Sciences
Orthopedics, Rheumatology, Trauma.title: Orthopedics, Rheumatology, Trauma
concepts: Common joint disorders (e.g., osteoarthritis, rheumatoid arthritis)
Approach to a patient with joint pain
objectives: Differentiate between different types of fractures.
Recognize the clinical presentation of common rheumatologic diseases.
Perform a basic musculoskeletal examination.
Prioritize care for a patient with multiple traumatic injuries.

Dermatology, Plastic Surgery/Burns.subject: Clinical Sciences
Dermatology, Plastic Surgery/Burns.title: Dermatology, Plastic Surgery/Burns
objectives: Identify and describe primary and secondary skin lesions.
Formulate a differential diagnosis for a patient with a rash.
Assess the severity of burn injuries.
Understand the principles of wound care.

Reproductive System II.subject: Clinical Sciences
Reproductive System II.title: Reproductive System II
objectives: Identify common STIs and their treatments.
Counsel patients on various contraceptive options.
Differentiate between common gynecological conditions.
Recognize the signs of prostate cancer.

Neurosciences and Psychiatry.subject: Clinical Sciences
Neurosciences and Psychiatry.title: Neurosciences and Psychiatry
objectives: Perform a comprehensive neurological exam.
Understand the acute management of a stroke patient.
Differentiate between major psychiatric disorders.
Explain the mechanisms of action for antidepressants and anxiolytics.

ENT (Otorhinolaryngology).subject: Clinical Sciences
ENT (Otorhinolaryngology).title: ENT (Otorhinolaryngology)
concepts: Anatomy of the ear, nose, and throat
objectives: Perform a basic otoscopic and nasal speculum examination.
Formulate a differential diagnosis for a patient with hearing loss.
Understand the causes and treatments of sinusitis.
Differentiate between viral and bacterial pharyngitis.

Ophthalmology.subject: Clinical Sciences
Ophthalmology.title: Ophthalmology
concepts: Anatomy and physiology of the eye
objectives: Perform a basic eye exam.
Explain the pathophysiology of glaucoma and cataracts.
Recognize the signs of retinal detachment.
Identify common ophthalmic emergencies.

Spiral-3 Modules.subject: Integrated Clinical Training
Spiral-3 Modules.title: Spiral-3: Supervised Practical Training (Year 5)
objectives: Demonstrate proficiency in taking a comprehensive patient history.
Perform and interpret a physical examination.
Develop and implement evidence-based management plans.
Communicate effectively with patients and the healthcare team.

Medicine Rotation.subject: Integrated Clinical Training
Medicine Rotation.title: Medicine
objectives: Manage common acute and chronic medical conditions.
Interpret laboratory and imaging results.
Provide compassionate care to patients with life-limiting illnesses.
Formulate a comprehensive management plan for a complex patient.

Pediatrics Rotation.subject: Integrated Clinical Training
Pediatrics Rotation.title: Pediatrics
objectives: Assess a child's growth and development.
Differentiate between common pediatric illnesses.
Understand the importance of vaccination.
Recognize and manage a pediatric emergency.

Cardiology Rotation.subject: Integrated Clinical Training
Cardiology Rotation.title: Cardiology
objectives: Interpret a complex ECG.
Manage a patient presenting with chest pain.
Understand the pathophysiology of heart failure.
Recognize the clinical signs of valvular heart disease.

Neurology Rotation.subject: Integrated Clinical Training
Neurology Rotation.title: Neurology
objectives: Perform a detailed neurological exam.
Identify the signs and symptoms of a stroke.
Develop a management plan for a patient with a seizure.
Understand the pathophysiology of neurodegenerative diseases.

Surgery Rotation.subject: Integrated Clinical Training
Surgery Rotation.title: Surgery
concepts: Fluid and electrolyte management in surgery
objectives: Assess a patient's fitness for surgery.
Manage a patient in the postoperative period.
Recognize and manage common surgical emergencies.
Understand the principles of surgical asepsis.

Gynecology and Obstetrics Rotation.subject: Integrated Clinical Training
Gynecology and Obstetrics Rotation.title: Gynecology and Obstetrics
objectives: Conduct a basic prenatal visit.
Manage a patient during labor and delivery.
Counsel patients on family planning.
Diagnose and treat common gynecological infections.
